title: URL.createObjectURL never revoked in PromptInput — unbounded memory leak
description:
## Issue
prompt-input.tsx creates object URLs for image attachments but never revokes them. A user attaching ten 5MB screenshots leaves 50MB of blobs pinned in the browser's URL registry until tab close.

## Origin
Pre-existing issue found during review of aurora-design-system-gms. Not introduced by that bead. Does not block gms from closing.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Memory grows without bound; invisible to user.

## Location
registry/aurora/blocks/ai/prompt-input/prompt-input.tsx lines 304-309

## Fix
Track URLs in a ref, revoke on remove and on unmount:
```tsx
const objectUrlsRef = React.useRef<string[]>([])
// revoke in removeMention + useEffect cleanup
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] URL.revokeObjectURL called when attachment removed
- [ ] URL.revokeObjectURL called for all remaining URLs on component unmount
- [ ] Test added covering this scenario
- [ ] Test fails before the fix, passes after

title: Document badgeVariants removal as breaking change in registry changelog
description:
## Issue
badge.tsx removes the `badgeVariants` CVA export. No live TypeScript source in this repo imports it. However this is a design-system registry package — external consumers who destructure `badgeVariants` for composition will get runtime `undefined` after upgrading with no compile-time error.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Silent breaking change for downstream registry consumers.

## Location
registry/aurora/ui/badge.tsx — removed `export { Badge, badgeVariants }`, now `export { Badge }`

## Why This Matters
External consumers using `import { badgeVariants } from 'aurora/badge'` for className composition will break at runtime without any TypeScript error on their side.

## Fix
- Add BREAKING CHANGE note to PR description / changelog
- Consider exporting an empty-stub or deprecation shim for one version
- Verify no external consumers in any downstream repos

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] PR description / changelog explicitly calls out removal of badgeVariants
- [ ] Version bump to indicate breaking change
- [ ] Test added covering this scenario according to project test standards
- [ ] Test fails before the fix, passes after

title: Fix inline color-mix() in mention chips — violates Aurora token contract
description:
## Issue
prompt-input.tsx lines 539-540 compute mention chip styling using inline `color-mix()`:

```tsx
background: "color-mix(in srgb, var(--aurora-accent-violet) 12%, transparent)",
border: "1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--aurora-accent-violet) 30%, transparent)",
```

Aurora's locked contract is: components consume pre-computed CSS tokens only — no inline color-mix() in component code. The tokens `--aurora-accent-violet-surface` (12% mix) and `--aurora-accent-violet-border` (32% mix) already exist in aurora.css.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Introduced code violates core architectural invariant; will silently drift when CSS token percentages are adjusted.

## Location
registry/aurora/blocks/ai/prompt-input/prompt-input.tsx lines 539-540

## Why This Matters
The entire point of pre-computing tokens in aurora.css is that color-mix() logic lives in one place. Inline color-mix() duplicates that logic and will get out of sync.

## Fix
```tsx
background: "var(--aurora-accent-violet-surface)",
border: "1px solid var(--aurora-accent-violet-border)",
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No inline color-mix() referencing aurora tokens in any component file
- [ ] Build passes after change
- [ ] Test added covering this scenario according to project test standards
- [ ] Test fails before the fix, passes after

title: Fix marketplace layout
description: Investigate the marketplace gallery page issues shown in the screenshot and fix the underlying layout/interaction problems in the demo without removing the route.

title: Reorganize gallery groups
description: Remove the Shadcn parity and AI elements group headers from the gallery navigation and reassign all of those pages into logical existing or new groups without deleting any routes.

title: Polish tool demos
description: Apply the compact tool styling to /gallery/tool-calls, tighten surrounding demo copy for tool and reasoning, and clarify whether tool and tool-calls remain distinct routes.

title: Simplify tool-call rows
description: Reduce tool-call UI to compact icon-plus-toolname rows with expandable input/output, removing the oversized timeline/card treatment.

title: Polish AI elements and parity surfaces
description: Address the full UI feedback pass across AI elements, parity components, gallery frontmatter typography, brand lockup, and shared control usage. This includes dropdown/button/textarea adoption, compact conversation and tool-call patterns, copy actions, syntax highlighting, context display corrections, attachment close icon positioning, parity polish for toggle/textarea and other shadcn parity components, deduping overlapping components, and aligning brand/gallery presentation with the Aurora design language.

title: Fix Thinking style tag deduplication with React 19 href+precedence props
description:
## Issue
Thinking injects KEYFRAMES via bare <style>{KEYFRAMES}</style>. Multiple Thinking instances (chat history) produce multiple identical <style> nodes in the DOM. React 19 can deduplicate stylesheet injections but only when href and precedence props are provided.

## Origin
Pre-existing issue found during review of aurora-design-system-gms.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — DOM pollution; grows with chat history length.

## Location
registry/aurora/blocks/ai/thinking/thinking.tsx line 583

## Fix
```tsx
<style href="aurora-thinking-keyframes" precedence="default">{KEYFRAMES}</style>
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Multiple Thinking instances produce only one <style> tag in <head>
- [ ] Keyframe animations still work

title: Fix toolbar / and @ buttons that never open command popup (regex mismatch)
description:
## Issue
Toolbar buttons that append '/' or '@' to the input value trigger onChange(value + '/'), but the detection regex requires whitespace before the slash: /(?:^|\s)\/(\w*)$/. When value is non-empty, 'describe the problem/' has no whitespace before '/', regex fails, popup never opens.

## Origin
Pre-existing issue found during review of aurora-design-system-gms.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Core affordance is broken; toolbar slash/mention buttons are non-functional.

## Location
registry/aurora/blocks/ai/prompt-input/prompt-input.tsx lines 717-749

## Fix
Append separator before trigger character:
```tsx
const sep = value.length > 0 && !value.endsWith(' ') ? ' ' : ''
onChange(value + sep + '/')
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Clicking / button opens slash command popup regardless of cursor position
- [ ] Clicking @ button opens mention popup regardless of cursor position
- [ ] Test added covering this scenario

title: Fix uncancellable onBlur setTimeout in PromptInput — fires after unmount
description:
## Issue
PromptInput onBlur fires a 150ms setTimeout to close popups. If the component unmounts during those 150ms (navigation, route change), the callback fires against a dead component.

## Origin
Pre-existing issue found during review of aurora-design-system-gms.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Timer accumulates on focus/blur churn; fires after unmount.

## Location
registry/aurora/blocks/ai/prompt-input/prompt-input.tsx lines 656-664

## Fix
Store timer id in ref, clear in useEffect cleanup. Or prevent blur via onMouseDown={(e) => e.preventDefault()} on popup items.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Timer is cleared on component unmount
- [ ] No setState calls on unmounted component
- [ ] Test added covering this scenario

title: Precompute boxShadow strings in tone maps to eliminate per-render allocations
description:
## Issue
Badge, Callout, and StatusIndicator create template literal strings for boxShadow on every render:
- badge.tsx: `0 0 4px ${dotColor}`
- callout.tsx: `inset 3px 0 0 ${accent}`, `0 0 10px ${accent}`
- status-indicator.tsx: `0 0 10px ${color}`

Since dotColor/accent/color come from module-level constant maps, the shadow strings can be precomputed once.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — At scale (200+ Badge/StatusIndicator instances in a table), per-render allocations accumulate. Also prevents effective React.memo usage.

## Location
registry/aurora/ui/badge.tsx, callout.tsx, status-indicator.tsx

## Fix
Add dotShadow/accentShadow/colorShadow fields to each tone map, computed once at module load:
```ts
info: { ..., dotShadow: '0 0 4px var(--aurora-info)' }
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No template literal strings in component render functions for shadow values
- [ ] Tone maps contain precomputed shadow strings
- [ ] Build passes

title: Narrow resolveTone parameter from string to BadgeTone | 'default' | undefined
description:
## Issue
resolveTone accepts `string | null | undefined` but all actual call sites pass `BadgeTone | 'default' | undefined`. The wide signature implies arbitrary runtime strings are expected but they are not — any unknown string silently falls to 'neutral' with no compile-time protection.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Type safety gap; silently swallows invalid variant values.

## Location
registry/aurora/ui/badge.tsx lines 60-63

## Fix
```tsx
function resolveTone(variant: BadgeTone | 'default' | undefined): BadgeTone {
  if (!variant || variant === 'default') return 'neutral'
  return variant  // already narrowed to BadgeTone
}
```
No cast needed. The in-operator fallback is removed because TypeScript now ensures only valid values reach this branch.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] resolveTone signature matches actual call-site types
- [ ] No unnecessary type casts in the function body
- [ ] TypeScript build passes with strict mode

title: Migrate CoT and Plan variants in thinking.tsx to AI_ACCENT
description:
## Issue
thinking.tsx introduces AI_ACCENT = 'var(--aurora-accent-violet)' and correctly migrates ThinkingBlock. But CotBlock and PlanBlock still use var(--aurora-accent-primary) for their header icons (lines 337-338, 478, 527).

A consumer rendering type='thinking' sees violet; switching to type='cot' or type='plan' reverts to cyan. The AI_ACCENT constant exists to prevent this exact inconsistency.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Visual inconsistency within the same component family.

## Location
registry/aurora/blocks/ai/thinking/thinking.tsx lines 337, 338, 478, 527

## Fix
Replace var(--aurora-accent-primary) with AI_ACCENT on lines 337, 338, 478.
For line 527 (6% mix background), use a pre-computed token or --aurora-accent-violet-surface.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] All three Thinking variants (thinking/cot/plan) use consistent violet AI accent
- [ ] AI_ACCENT constant used throughout — no var(--aurora-accent-primary) for AI-identity cues

title: Add pulse default for automating tone in StatusIndicator
description:
## Issue
StatusIndicator defaults pulse to `tone === 'syncing'`. The `automating` tone is semantically in-flight (on par with syncing) but does not receive the pulse default. Callers must pass `pulse` manually or get a static dot for an active AI process.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Ergonomic inconsistency; violates principle of least surprise for AI-automation states.

## Location
registry/aurora/ui/status-indicator.tsx line 27

## Fix
```tsx
pulse = tone === "syncing" || tone === "automating"
```
Or if intentionally static for automating, add a comment documenting the decision.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] automating tone pulses by default OR decision is documented in code

title: Export toneColor from status-indicator and remove duplicate in timeline
description:
## Issue
toneColor is defined identically in both status-indicator.tsx and timeline.tsx. Both files were modified in this PR (both have `automating` added). This PR demonstrates the failure mode: any new StatusTone addition requires two simultaneous edits with no compile-time guarantee of sync.

```tsx
// timeline.tsx already imports from status-indicator:
import type { StatusTone } from "./status-indicator"
// It can also import toneColor:
import { toneColor } from "./status-indicator"
```

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Maintenance risk; next tone addition will require coordinated two-file edit.

## Location
registry/aurora/ui/timeline.tsx lines 7-15 (remove)
registry/aurora/ui/status-indicator.tsx line 8 (add export keyword)

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] toneColor exported from status-indicator.tsx
- [ ] timeline.tsx imports toneColor instead of re-declaring
- [ ] Build and type-check pass

title: Expand Aurora color token semantics
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: define an expanded Aurora color token contract covering brand, semantic state, and interaction tokens; document the design; and scope the gallery/demo rollout so implementation can follow a reviewed spec.
notes: Implementation plan drafted at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-11-aurora-color-token-expansion.md; waiting on user execution choice.

title: Implement Aurora registry taxonomy
description: Implement the hybrid registry taxonomy from docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-11-aurora-registry-taxonomy-design.md: move style and block source paths, update registry metadata, regenerate public registry payloads, update docs, and verify lint/build gates.
close_reason: Implemented registry taxonomy, regenerated public registry payloads, updated docs, and verified lint/composition/build gates.

title: Define Aurora registry taxonomy
description: Write and record a design for organizing the Aurora shadcn registry around a hybrid primitive-plus-agent-app structure. Capture recommended top-level directories, domain groups under blocks, and migration guidance for docs and discovery.
design: Recommended hybrid taxonomy: keep ui/styles/hooks/icons flat and organize product-facing blocks by domain.

title: Polish marketplace states
description: Review the marketplace's secondary states after the recent layout cleanup, especially table view, active source filtering, empty results, and detail sheet presentation. Tighten any remaining rough UI or interaction edges and land the next refinement pass.

title: Continue marketplace visual refinement
description: The marketplace gallery demo is improved but still needs another visual polish pass. Review the live desktop and mobile states, refine spacing, hierarchy, card density, and filter/sidebar presentation, then land the next iteration.

title: Polish marketplace gallery demo
description: The marketplace gallery route still looks under-polished after the overflow fix. Tighten hierarchy, layout density, interaction affordances, and responsive behavior for the marketplace block and its demo so the page feels intentional and production-ready.

title: Condense reasoning summary
description: Reduce the reasoning UI to a compact icon plus label treatment instead of a wide column-sized panel, while keeping the content expandable.

title: Refine tool-calls layout
description: Update the tool-calls component so it does not reserve a wide tool-name column. Use a compact icon-first expandable pattern and align the styling more closely with the timeline component.

title: Polish flagged gallery components
description: Audit and improve the gallery components the user flagged: native-select, breadcrumb, menubar, table/tables, sheet/drawer, ask-user-question mobile overflow, and related UI polish issues found during browser review. Make the demos functional where requested and reduce incorrect or over-assumed UI treatments.

title: Fix brand demo wordmark description
description: Update the brand demo copy so it matches the corrected Labby wordmark treatment: the last two letters use the blue accent, not a pink y.

title: Correct Labby wordmark accent colors
description: Update the Labby wordmark component so the last two letters use the intended blue accent treatment instead of only the final letter using pink.

title: Audit and polish Aurora gallery components
description: Audit the live aurora.tootie.tv gallery using agent-browser, identify visual and interaction polish issues across served component pages, and implement the corresponding UI fixes in this repo.

title: Implement shadcn registry improvements
description: Implement the registry improvements identified during review: replace placeholder registry identity, fix dependency metadata drift, add item metadata fields (author/categories/docs/meta), add explicit target paths where install location matters, add cssVars/css metadata for Tailwind v4-friendly token installation, add branded/content-negotiated registry hosting plus namespace guidance, and version external dependencies consistently.

title: Use Object.hasOwn instead of in operator in resolveTone and tone map lookups
description:
## Issue
badge.tsx uses `(variant as BadgeTone) in badgeToneMap` which traverses the prototype chain. Prototype-inherited keys like 'constructor'/'toString'/'hasOwnProperty' pass the check. The result is undefined fields destructured from Function objects, producing 'undefined' in CSS shadow strings.

Same issue with direct unguarded indexing in callout.tsx, status-indicator.tsx, timeline.tsx.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — No XSS/injection risk; worst case is styling defect with invalid keys.

## Location
registry/aurora/ui/badge.tsx line 62, callout.tsx, status-indicator.tsx, timeline.tsx

## Fix
```tsx
return Object.hasOwn(badgeToneMap, variant) ? variant as BadgeTone : 'neutral'
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Object.hasOwn used instead of in operator for tone map lookups
- [ ] Build passes

title: Update Aurora registry URLs
description: Update local config and docs to use the deployed aurora.tootie.tv registry URLs instead of raw GitHub URLs, keeping namespaced installs pointed at /r/{name}.json and documenting the branded root URL behavior.
